basicDetails.schemeName: Documentation of Tiatr through Research Work

schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to encourage research work in the field of Tiatr and facilitate writers to take up research work in different aspects of Tiatr, its Song & Music. Research work will be published in book form with remuneration provided to researchers.
schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme "Documentation of Tiatr through Research Work" was launched by the Tiatr Academy under the Art, Culture and Museums Department, Government of Goa. The scheme aims to encourage research work in the field of Tiatr and facilitate writers to take up research work in different aspects of Tiatr, its Song & Music. The scheme provides publication of research work in book form with a minimum of 500 copies printed and remuneration as fixed by TAG as per the researcher's experience. The applicant must be a researcher interested in writing books on tiatr after doing thorough research and must submit three typed copies of the manuscript for approval. The scheme is implemented by the Tiatr Academy of Goa (TAG). The applications for this scheme are accepted offline.
schemeContent.benefits_md: > ***Publication Benefits*** - TAG will publish the research work in book form after thorough research on the subject. - The publication may be in English or Konkani (Roman Script). - TAG will print a minimum of 500 copies of the book. - 100 copies will be given to the writer. - The remaining copies will be used by TAG for various purposes. > ***Financial Benefits*** The author/researcher will be given remuneration as fixed by TAG as per his/her experience for the research work undertaken. > ***Editorial Support*** - The manuscript approved by the Recommendation Board shall be edited by a member of Editorial Board. - Editing will include checking for spelling and orthography. ***Conditions*** _*The manuscript received from the author/researcher will be considered for publication only after getting approval from the Recommendation Board appointed by TAG._ _*The author/researcher will have to provide TAG, 3 (three) typed copies of the manuscript._ _*If approved, the author/researcher will have to submit a soft copy of the manuscript on a Compact Disc/Digital Versatile Disc/pen drive._

children.text: The scheme "Documentation of Tiatr through Research Work" was launched by the Tiatr Academy under the Art, Culture and Museums Department, Government of Goa. The scheme aims to encourage research work in the field of Tiatr and facilitate writers to take up research work in different aspects of Tiatr, its Song & Music. The scheme provides publication of research work in book form with a minimum of 500 copies printed and remuneration as fixed by TAG as per the researcher's experience. The applicant must be a researcher interested in writing books on tiatr after doing thorough research and must submit three typed copies of the manuscript for approval. The scheme is implemented by the Tiatr Academy of Goa (TAG). The applications for this scheme are accepted offline.
Publication Benefits
TAG will publish the research work in book form after thorough research on the subject.
The publication may be in English or Konkani (Roman Script).
TAG will print a minimum of 500 copies of the book.
100 copies will be given to the writer.
The remaining copies will be used by TAG for various purposes.
Financial Benefits
The author/researcher will be given remuneration as fixed by TAG as per his/her experience for the research work undertaken.
Editorial Support
The manuscript approved by the Recommendation Board shall be edited by a member of Editorial Board.
Editing will include checking for spelling and orthography.
Conditions
*The manuscript received from the author/researcher will be considered for publication only after getting approval from the Recommendation Board appointed by TAG.
*The author/researcher will have to provide TAG, 3 (three) typed copies of the manuscript.
*If approved, the author/researcher will have to submit a soft copy of the manuscript on a Compact Disc/Digital Versatile Disc/pen drive.
Step 1:
The interested applicant should take a printout of the
prescribed format
of the application form.
Step 2:

In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields: name of the applicant, complete address, contact number, email address, Aadhaar Card number, Permanent Account Number Card number, name of the research, and aspects of tiatr on which research is done. Attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required). Sign the application form and mention the date.
Step 3:
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to -
Tiatr Academy of Goa, AS-1 / AS-4, Block A, 2nd Floor, Campal Trade Centre, Opposite Kala Academy, Campal, Panaji, Goa, India – 403001
*Applicants are advised to save a copy of the submitted application form and any acknowledgment
Post-Application Processes
Step 1:
Recommendation Board Review

The submitted manuscript will be reviewed by the Recommendation Board consisting of 2 members appointed by TAG. The board members will be paid a suitable honorarium as fixed by TAG for the work undertaken.
Step 2:
Approval and Soft Copy Submission
If the manuscript is approved by the Recommendation Board, submit a soft copy of the manuscript on a Compact Disc/Digital Versatile Disc/pen drive to the Tiatr Academy of Goa.
Step 3:
Editorial Process
The approved manuscript will undergo editing by a member of the Editorial Board, which includes checking for spelling and orthography.
Step 4:
Publication and Remuneration

Upon completion of the editorial process, TAG will proceed with printing a minimum of 500 copies of the book and provide remuneration to the author/researcher as per the fixed rates based on experience.

applicationProcess.process_md:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should take a printout of the [prescribed format](https://www.goa.gov.in/wp-content/uploads/2021/06/Citizen-Charter-TAG.pdf) of the application form. **Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields: name of the applicant, complete address, contact number, email address, Aadhaar Card number, Permanent Account Number Card number, name of the research, and aspects of tiatr on which research is done. Attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required). Sign the application form and mention the date. **Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to - ***Tiatr Academy of Goa, AS-1 / AS-4, Block A, 2nd Floor, Campal Trade Centre, Opposite Kala Academy, Campal, Panaji, Goa, India – 403001*** _*Applicants are advised to save a copy of the submitted application form and any acknowledgment_
> ***Post-Application Processes*** **Step 1:** _Recommendation Board Review_ The submitted manuscript will be reviewed by the Recommendation Board consisting of 2 members appointed by TAG. The board members will be paid a suitable honorarium as fixed by TAG for the work undertaken. **Step 2:** _Approval and Soft Copy Submission_ If the manuscript is approved by the Recommendation Board, submit a soft copy of the manuscript on a Compact Disc/Digital Versatile Disc/pen drive to the Tiatr Academy of Goa. **Step 3:** _Editorial Process_ The approved manuscript will undergo editing by a member of the Editorial Board, which includes checking for spelling and orthography. **Step 4:** _Publication and Remuneration_ Upon completion of the editorial process, TAG will proceed with printing a minimum of 500 copies of the book and provide remuneration to the author/researcher as per the fixed rates based on experience.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: - The applicant must be a researcher interested in writing books on tiatr after doing thorough research on the subject. - Preference will be given to any researcher who has already done research on any aspect of Tiatr, its Song & Music.

children.text: The applicant must be a researcher interested in writing books on tiatr after doing thorough research on the subject.
Preference will be given to any researcher who has already done research on any aspect of Tiatr, its Song & Music.
